goal: Bring th/index.html to SEO/AEO structural parity with the already-live EN index.html prototype (recap: redesign_seo_recap.md)
done: Applied the 4 recap fixes to th/index.html only: (1) injected one visually-hidden canonical <h1> at top of <main> and demoted both visual headers (mobile .home-wordmark + desktop .mtd-h1) from <h1> to <h2> keeping classes so styling is byte-identical -> page now has exactly one <h1>; (2) restructured <main class=page-shell> into <main id=main-content> wrapping an inner <div class=page-shell home-page> so one <main> wraps both mobile + desktop blocks; (3) converted desktop reading-modes grid <div class=mtd-modes> -> <nav class=mtd-modes aria-label=Reading modes>; (4) head untouched (WebApplication + BreadcrumbList + 2x ImageObject JSON-LD, 3 hreflang, canonical preserved). EN index.html already had these (the prototype) so it was left unchanged.
verified: Tag balance th/index.html div 120/120, main 1/1, nav 2/2, section 7/7, h1 count=1. No tag-qualified CSS selectors on the affected classes (grep) so styling unaffected. Headless Chrome render at 1440px desktop + 390px mobile: both visually identical to before.
